 Employee Travel and Expense Policy
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This policy describes travel approvals, booking rules, reimbursement requirements, meal limits, and
expense claim timelines.
Employees must use reasonable judgment and choose cost-effective travel options for business trips.
Travel Approval
Domestic travel must be approved by the reporting manager before booking.
International travel must be approved by the department head and finance team before tickets are
purchased.
Emergency travel may be approved through email confirmation and regularized after the trip.
Booking Rules
Economy class must be used for domestic flights. Premium travel requires written department-head
approval.
Hotel bookings should follow the approved city-wise budget limits unless the client or event location

avel requires written department-head
approval.
Hotel bookings should follow the approved city-wise budget limits unless the client or event location
requires an exception.
Reimbursement
Expense claims must be submitted within ten working days after travel completion.
Original bills or digital receipts are required for hotel, flight, taxi, and meal expenses.
Claims without receipts may be rejected unless finance approves a documented exception.
Expense Type
Required Proof
Flight or Train
Ticket and invoice
Hotel
Tax invoice and payment receipt
Taxi or Rideshare
Trip receipt
Meals
Bill with date and location
Meal and Local Transport Limits
Meal reimbursement is allowed for business travel days only and must follow the company expense
limits.
Local transport should use standard taxi, rideshare, bus, metro, or approved rental services.

--- Page 1 ---
Universal RAG Test Corpus - Page 1
 Product Warranty and Returns Guide
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This guide explains product warranty coverage, return eligibility, replacement conditions, refund
timelines, and customer support process.
Warranty and returns are handled according to product category, purchase proof, issue type, and
usage condition.
Warranty Coverage
Standard electronic products include a one-year limited warranty from the date of purchase.
Accessories such as cables, chargers, and adapters include a six-month limited warranty unless
otherwise mentioned on the invoice.
Warranty covers manufacturing defects, power failure caused by internal faults, and non-physical
component malfunction.
Product Category
Warranty Period
Electronics
One year limited warranty
Accessories
Six months limited warranty
Replacement Un

l
component malfunction.
Product Category
Warranty Period
Electronics
One year limited warranty
Accessories
Six months limited warranty
Replacement Unit
Remaining original warranty or ninety days, whichever is longer
Return Eligibility
Customers may request a return within seven calendar days of delivery if the product is unused,
undamaged, and includes original packaging.
Opened products may be eligible for replacement if a verified manufacturing defect is found during
inspection.
Refund Timeline
Approved refunds are initiated within five to seven business days after the returned product passes
inspection.
Refunds are sent to the original payment method. Bank processing time may vary by payment
provider.
Warranty Exclusions
Warranty does not cover physical damage, water damage, unauthorized repair, misuse, normal wear
and tear, or missing serial numbers.
Products without valid purchase proof may be rejected for warranty service.

g serial numbers.
Products without valid purchase proof may be rejected for warranty service.

Universal RAG Test Corpus - Page 1
 IT Security and Data Protection Policy
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This policy defines security practices for account access, passwords, devices, data handling, and
incident reporting.
All employees and contractors must protect company data and follow approved security controls.
Password Rules
Passwords must be at least twelve characters long and must not be reused across personal and
company accounts.
Multi-factor authentication is mandatory for email, code repositories, cloud platforms, and production
systems.
Device Security
Company laptops must use screen lock, disk encryption, antivirus protection, and operating system
updates.
Lost or stolen devices must be reported to IT within one hour of discovery.
Data Classification
Public data can be shared externally without restriction. Intern

olen devices must be reported to IT within one hour of discovery.
Data Classification
Public data can be shared externally without restriction. Internal data is limited to employees and
approved contractors.
Confidential data includes customer information, employee records, contracts, API keys, credentials,
and financial reports.
Restricted data must not be copied to personal devices, personal email, public drives, or unapproved
AI tools.
Classification
Examples
Public
Marketing brochures, public website text
Internal
Team notes, internal process documents
Confidential
Customer records, contracts, API keys
Restricted
Production secrets, regulated personal data
Incident Reporting
Suspected phishing, unauthorized access, malware, or accidental data exposure must be reported
immediately to the IT security team.
Employees should not delete evidence or attempt private investigation before reporting a security
incident.

idence or attempt private investigation before reporting a security
incident.

--- Page 1 ---
Universal RAG Test Corpus - Page 1
 HR Leave and Attendance Policy
Purpose: This file is part of a sample multi-document project for testing API-key protected RAG upload, indexing,
semantic search, chat answers, and source citations.
Overview
This policy explains working hours, attendance expectations, leave categories, approval rules, and
documentation requirements for employees.
Employees are expected to record attendance accurately every working day. Missing attendance
records must be regularized within three working days.
Working Hours
Standard working hours are 9:30 AM to 6:30 PM from Monday to Friday. A flexible grace period of 15
minutes is allowed up to three times in a month.
Repeated late login beyond the grace limit may be marked as half-day leave after manager review.
Leave Types
Casual leave can be used for short personal needs and should be requested at least one day in
advance whenever possible.
Sick leave can be taken for health reasons. If sick leave exc

rt personal needs and should be requested at least one day in
advance whenever possible.
Sick leave can be taken for health reasons. If sick leave exceeds two consecutive working days, a
medical certificate may be requested.
Earned leave must be planned and approved at least seven days before the start date unless an
exception is approved by HR.
Leave Type
Summary
Casual Leave
Short personal needs; advance notice preferred.
Sick Leave
Health reasons; certificate may be needed after two days.
Earned Leave
Planned leave; seven-day advance approval recommended.
Approval Rules
All leave requests must be submitted through the official HR portal or approved communication
channel.
Managers should approve or reject leave requests within two working days. HR will review policy
exceptions.
Attendance Regularization
Employees may regularize missed punches, incorrect login times, or approved work-from-home
attendance within three working days.
Regularization requests must include a clear reason an

unches, incorrect login times, or approved work-from-home
attendance within three working days.
Regularization requests must include a clear reason and manager confirmation.

This Privacy Policy explains how Medi-Scan collects, uses, stores, shares, and protects personal information,
health information, medical documents, location information, payment-related information, and app usage data
when users access Medi-Scan mobile application services.
Important: Medi-Scan is not a substitute for a doctor, pharmacist, hospital, laboratory, emergency service,
or licensed healthcare professional. AI explanations and suggestions are informational only.

--- Page 3 ---
Medi-Scan Privacy Policy
Page 3
1. Introduction
This Privacy Policy explains how Medi-Scan ("Medi-Scan", "we", "our", or "us") collects, uses, stores, shares, and
protects information when you use our mobile application, website, AI assistant, document scanning tools,
medication reminder system, emergency alert features, and related services collectively referred to as the
"Services".
Medi-Scan is an AI-powered smart health companion designed to help users digitize prescriptions, understand
blood test reports, manage medicine reminders, maintain health records, calculate BMI, track medication
adherence, receive diet suggestions, and ask health-related questions through MediBot.
2. Medical Disclaimer
Medi-Scan provides informational and organizational support only. It does not provide medical diagnosis,
treatment, prescription decisions, emergency care, or professional medical advice.
AI-generated report explanations, prescription summaries, safety checks, dietary rec

iption decisions, emergency care, or professional medical advice.
AI-generated report explanations, prescription summaries, safety checks, dietary recommendations, and MediBot
responses may be incomplete or inaccurate. You must consult a qualified doctor, pharmacist, or healthcare
provider before making medical decisions, changing medication, stopping medication, or relying on health
suggestions.
In a medical emergency, contact local emergency services immediately.
3. Definitions and Scope
Personal Data means information that identifies or can reasonably identify an individual, such as name, email
address, phone number, account details, device information, or location information.
Health Data means information related to physical or medical condition, prescriptions, medicines, dosage, lab
reports, diagnostic values, BMI, weight, symptoms, medicine history, allergies, health profile, or wellness data.
Medical Documents means prescriptions, blood test reports, lab report PDFs, images, do

medicine history, allergies, health profile, or wellness data.
Medical Documents means prescriptions, blood test reports, lab report PDFs, images, doctor notes, medical
slips, and other health-related files uploaded or scanned through Medi-Scan.
This Policy applies to Medi-Scan users who access or use our Services. It does not apply to third-party websites,
hospitals, labs, doctors, pharmacies, payment providers, or external services that have their own privacy policies.
4. Information We Collect
Medi-Scan collects information necessary to provide health document scanning, medication tracking, reminders,
emergency alerts, AI assistance, and account security.
4.1 Account and Contact Information
●
Name, email address, phone number, OTP verification details, account preferences, login activity, and support
communications.
●
Device identifiers and security logs used to protect your account and prevent unauthorized access.
4.2 Health and Medical Information
●
Prescription images, extracted

rs and security logs used to protect your account and prevent unauthorized access.
4.2 Health and Medical Information
●
Prescription images, extracted medicine names, dosages, timing, frequency, doctor instructions, and critical
medicine settings.
●
Blood test reports, lab markers, diagnostic values, report summaries, trends, and possible concern indicators.

--- Page 4 ---
Medi-Scan Privacy Policy
Page 4
●
Medical history, health conditions, allergies, supplements, vitamins, adherence records, missed dose history,
BMI, height, weight, and diet-related preferences that you choose to provide.
4.3 Documents, Camera, Gallery, and Files
●
Prescription photos, lab report images, PDF reports, uploaded records, and extracted text from those
documents.
●
Camera, gallery, and file access are used only when you choose to scan or upload a medical document.
4.4 Reminder, Notification, and App Usage Information
●
Medicine schedules, reminder times, snooze actions, Take Now confirmations, missed dos

cument.
4.4 Reminder, Notification, and App Usage Information
●
Medicine schedules, reminder times, snooze actions, Take Now confirmations, missed doses, adherence
streaks, and calendar progress.
●
App version, device model, operating system, crash logs, error logs, feature usage events, and technical
diagnostics.
4.5 Emergency Contact Information
●
Emergency contact name, phone number, email address, relationship label if provided, critical medication
settings, and missed-dose alert history.
●
You are responsible for ensuring that your emergency contact agrees to receive emergency alerts from
Medi-Scan.
5. Location Usage
Medi-Scan may collect and use your location only when location-based features are enabled and permission is
granted by you. The primary location feature is emergency missed-dose alerting for critical medications.
If a critical dose is missed and emergency alerts are enabled, Medi-Scan may include your last known GPS
location or approximate location in the alert sent t

itical dose is missed and emergency alerts are enabled, Medi-Scan may include your last known GPS
location or approximate location in the alert sent to your selected emergency contact.
Medi-Scan does not use location for advertising. You can disable location permission through device settings, but
emergency missed-dose alerts may not include location after permission is disabled.
6. Payments
If Medi-Scan offers paid subscriptions, premium features, consultations, reports, or in-app purchases, payment
processing may be handled by third-party payment processors, app stores, or billing providers.
Medi-Scan does not store full credit card numbers, debit card numbers, CVV, UPI PINs, banking passwords, or
complete payment credentials on our own servers. Payment providers may collect billing name, payment method
details, transaction amount, invoice information, tax information, and payment status according to their own privacy
policies.
We may store limited payment-related records such as sub

ice information, tax information, and payment status according to their own privacy
policies.
We may store limited payment-related records such as subscription status, transaction ID, plan type, purchase
date, renewal date, refund status, and billing support communications to manage accounts, comply with legal
obligations, and provide customer support.
7. How We Use Information
We use collected information to operate, secure, personalize, and improve Medi-Scan.
●
Create and manage user accounts, verify OTP login, and provide customer support.

--- Page 5 ---
Medi-Scan Privacy Policy
Page 5
●
Scan prescriptions, extract medicine names, dosage, and timing, and allow user confirmation before saving.
●
Interpret blood test reports in plain language and track health trends over time.
●
Create medication reminders, full-screen alarms, snooze alerts, adherence logs, streaks, and calendar views.
●
Provide MediBot AI responses based on user-provided prescriptions, reports, and medical history.

erts, adherence logs, streaks, and calendar views.
●
Provide MediBot AI responses based on user-provided prescriptions, reports, and medical history.
●
Generate personalized diet suggestions and possible food-medicine interaction alerts.
●
Detect duplicate medicines, possible safety mismatches, and situations where expert review is recommended.
●
Send emergency missed-dose alerts when enabled by the user.
●
Improve app reliability, security, OCR quality, AI response quality, and user experience.
8. Data Sharing
We do not sell personal health information, prescriptions, medical reports, medication history, emergency contact
information, or location information. We share information only as needed to provide Medi-Scan services, protect
users, comply with law, or complete business operations.
8.1 Service Providers
We may share limited information with trusted providers that help us operate the app, including cloud hosting,
databases, OTP delivery, push notifications, AI/OCR processing, em

ation with trusted providers that help us operate the app, including cloud hosting,
databases, OTP delivery, push notifications, AI/OCR processing, email delivery, analytics, crash reporting,
customer support, security monitoring, and payment processing.
8.2 Emergency Contacts
If you enable emergency alerts, we may share missed critical dose information and last known location with your
selected emergency contact.
8.3 Healthcare Providers, Caregivers, or Family Members
We share health records with doctors, caregivers, family members, or other people only when you choose to share
them or use a feature that sends information to them.
8.4 Legal, Safety, and Business Transfers
We may disclose information if required by law, legal process, regulatory request, safety concern, fraud prevention
need, security incident, enforcement of our terms, merger, acquisition, restructuring, or sale of assets.
9. App Permissions
Permission
Purpose
Camera
To scan prescriptions, reports, and medical documen

erger, acquisition, restructuring, or sale of assets.
9. App Permissions
Permission
Purpose
Camera
To scan prescriptions, reports, and medical documents.
Photos, Gallery, Files, or Storage
To upload prescription images, PDFs, lab reports, and health records.
Notifications
To send medicine reminders, full-screen alarms, snooze alerts, and critical dose notifications.
Location
To include last known location in emergency missed-dose alerts, only when enabled and permitted.
Contacts
If used, to help select an emergency contact. Medi-Scan does not upload the full contact list unless clearly disclosed and consented to.
Internet
To sync account data, process AI features, deliver OTPs, store records securely, and operate cloud services.

--- Page 6 ---
Medi-Scan Privacy Policy
Page 6
You can manage app permissions through your device settings. Some features may not function properly if
required permissions are disabled.
10. Data Storage and Security
We use reasonable technical and organization

me features may not function properly if
required permissions are disabled.
10. Data Storage and Security
We use reasonable technical and organizational safeguards to protect user information, including secure
transmission, secure storage, OTP authentication, access controls, encryption where appropriate, monitoring, and
limited access to sensitive data.
Because no electronic system is completely secure, we cannot guarantee absolute security. You are responsible
for keeping your device, phone number, email account, and app access secure.
11. Data Retention
We retain information only for as long as necessary to provide Medi-Scan services, comply with law, resolve
disputes, prevent fraud or misuse, maintain backups, and improve security.
Medical records are retained until you delete them or close your account, unless longer retention is required or
permitted by law. Reminder history, adherence logs, emergency contact details, and location alerts are retained
only as needed for the releva

uired or
permitted by law. Reminder history, adherence logs, emergency contact details, and location alerts are retained
only as needed for the relevant features. Technical logs may be retained for a limited period for security and
debugging.
12. Account Deletion
You may request deletion of your Medi-Scan account through the in-app account deletion option, if available, or by
contacting us at [Insert Privacy / Support Email].
After a verified deletion request, we will delete or anonymize personal data, health data, uploaded documents,
medication history, reminder data, emergency contact details, and account information unless retention is required
for legal, security, fraud prevention, backup, dispute resolution, tax, accounting, or legitimate business purposes
permitted by law.
Some deleted information may remain in encrypted backups for a limited period before permanent removal.
13. Age Restriction and Children's Privacy
Medi-Scan is intended for users who are at least 18 years old.

a limited period before permanent removal.
13. Age Restriction and Children's Privacy
Medi-Scan is intended for users who are at least 18 years old. Users under 18 may use the Services only with the
involvement and consent of a parent or legal guardian, where permitted by law.
We do not knowingly collect personal or health information from children without appropriate consent. If we learn
that we collected such information without required consent, we will take reasonable steps to delete it.
Parents or guardians may contact us to request access, correction, or deletion of a child's data.
14. Legal, Compliance, and Safety
We may process or disclose information when necessary to comply with applicable laws, regulations, court orders,
government requests, tax or accounting requirements, safety obligations, fraud prevention, security investigations,
enforcement of terms, or protection of rights, property, and users.
Where applicable, Medi-Scan may process personal data in accordance with

gations,
enforcement of terms, or protection of rights, property, and users.
Where applicable, Medi-Scan may process personal data in accordance with data protection laws such as the
Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 in India and other applicable privacy laws based on user location.
For legal notices, privacy concerns, complaints, or data requests, contact: [Insert Legal / Privacy Email].

--- Page 7 ---
Medi-Scan Privacy Policy
Page 7
15. AI Processing and Health Insights
Medi-Scan uses AI technologies such as OCR, handwriting recognition, text extraction, natural language
processing, retrieval-based context, and AI-assisted summarization to support prescription scanning, lab report
explanation, MediBot responses, diet suggestions, duplicate medicine checks, and safety alerts.
Unless we separately ask for your permission, we do not use identifiable prescriptions, medical reports, health
records, or personal health data to train public AI models. We may use anonymized or aggre

not use identifiable prescriptions, medical reports, health
records, or personal health data to train public AI models. We may use anonymized or aggregated data to improve
reliability, safety, and performance where permitted by law.
AI results should be reviewed carefully. Users must verify extracted medicines, dosages, timings, report values,
and emergency contact settings before relying on them.
16. User Rights and Choices
Depending on applicable law and your location, you may have rights to access, correct, delete, export, restrict,
object to, or withdraw consent for certain processing of your personal data.
●
Access and review personal data and health records stored in your account.
●
Correct inaccurate account, medicine, report, or emergency contact information.
●
Delete uploaded records, prescriptions, reports, reminders, or your entire account.
●
Withdraw consent for optional features such as notifications, location sharing, emergency alerts, and AI
personalization.
●
Disable pe

tire account.
●
Withdraw consent for optional features such as notifications, location sharing, emergency alerts, and AI
personalization.
●
Disable permissions from device settings, including camera, files, notifications, location, and contacts.
●
Contact support for privacy requests, complaints, or account assistance.
17. Third-Party Services
Medi-Scan may use or link to third-party services such as cloud hosting providers, payment processors, app
stores, laboratories, doctors, pharmacies, analytics providers, AI/OCR providers, and support platforms.
Third-party services may have their own privacy policies and terms. Medi-Scan is not responsible for privacy
practices of services that we do not own or control.
18. Changes to This Policy
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. If we make significant changes, we will notify users through
the app, website, email, or another appropriate method. The updated policy will be effective from the date shown
at the top of this documen

rs through
the app, website, email, or another appropriate method. The updated policy will be effective from the date shown
at the top of this document.
19. Additional Notice for Users in India
If you are located in India, your personal data may be processed in accordance with applicable Indian privacy laws,
including the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 and applicable rules.
You may have rights to access information, correct personal data, request deletion, withdraw consent, raise
grievances, and nominate another person to exercise rights in case of death or incapacity, where applicable. To
exercise these rights, contact us at [Insert Privacy / Support Email].
